Redesign settings screen with Figma design system (#63)

Replace the old storage-focused settings view with a modern, card-based layout matching the Figma design. Features sections for general settings (notifications, language, save location), theme selection, clear history action, and helpful links (rate app, privacy, terms, version). Uses SF Symbols for consistency with the design system, orange accent color for interactions, and proper spacing/typography tokens.

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

MCP server: RAII ServeProcess, FileState consolidation, idle eviction

- ServeProcess owns stdin/pid/reader_handle with Drop that kills process
  group and joins reader thread, preventing restart cascade bugs
- Consolidate per-file state (version, diagnostics, progress, pending
  checks) into single FileState struct instead of separate HashMaps
- Add 10-minute idle file eviction via recv_timeout to free LSP memory
- Unified close_uri method for consistent file cleanup
- Remove monitor thread (reader EOF detection suffices for crash handling)
- Remove QueuedCheck/PendingCheck duplication
- Add debug_assert_active_count for active slot tracking
- Simplify sync_changed_files to drain pending_changes directly

commit.message:
fix: resolve LinkedIn URLs missing for all contacts

Root cause: three issues preventing LinkedIn URLs from appearing:
1. ZoomInfo contact search does not return linkedinUrl (engagement data)
2. _normalize_contact() had casing bug: tried "linkedInUrl" (capital I)
   but ZoomInfo returns "linkedinUrl" (lowercase i) - case-sensitive miss
3. ZoomInfo-only contacts had no LinkedIn enrichment source

Fixes:
- Fix casing in _normalize_contact to try "linkedinUrl" first
- Add Step 2.85: Apollo LinkedIn Enrichment - backfills LinkedIn URLs
  for contacts missing them via Apollo people/match by name + company
- Apollo contacts already have linkedin_url; merge preserves them

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(skills): add built-in excalidraw diagram skill (#751) (#752)

* feat(skills): add built-in excalidraw diagram skill with web UI rendering (#751)

Adds an excalidraw.yaml skill that instructs the AI to produce Excalidraw JSON,
a canvas.js Excalidraw renderer using iframe isolation with SVG export, CSS for
the Excalidraw canvas container, and the /excalidraw-viewer endpoint in app.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(services): comprehensive streaming stability and recovery hardening (#751)

SSE client: AbortController for in-flight streams, tool timer leak cleanup,
watchdog/queue error recovery, stream_done EventSource handler, beforeunload
and pagehide abort.

SSE server: per-conversation asyncio.Lock to prevent TOCTOU race, aclose()
on inner generator in keepalive wrapper with fallback cleanup, atomic
cancel event set cleanup, request reference removal for GC.

Anthropic provider: cancel-aware stream creation with hard timeout,
per-chunk stall detection via asyncio.wait, stream context manager cleanup.

Agent loop: hard 300s tool execution timeout, proactive message compaction
at 80 messages.

AI providers: token refresh recursion guard on all methods.

Event bus: bounded subscriber queues (maxsize=1000), poll loop
retry-with-backoff instead of permanent crash.

Events router: replaced 50ms busy-wait polling with asyncio.wait on
queue get tasks.

Subagent: child AIService httpx client close in finally block.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: apply ruff format fixes (#751)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(security): replace postMessage wildcard origins and update docs (#751)

- Replace '*' target origin with window.location.origin in postMessage calls
- Add SECURITY-REVIEW comment for unsafe-inline CSP in viewer iframe
- Add excalidraw to a-help built-in skills list
- Document excalidraw in CLAUDE.md canvas.py entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(routers): prevent task leak on SSE disconnect in events endpoint (#751)

Track active asyncio tasks and cancel them in the finally block to prevent
lingering queue.get() coroutines on client disconnect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Sprint 8 Intelligence Foundation — AST engine, framework profiles, ContextAnalyzer, compliance infra

Port of Sprint 8 engineering from kidsafe-dev to halo-public (canonical repo).

Core AST scanning infrastructure:
- ASTRuleEngine with 10 rule-specific analyzers (tracking-003, retention-005, ext-017,
  sec-015, auth-001, ui-008, ugc-014, flow-009, cookies-016, ETHICAL-001)
- ScopeAnalyzer: file-level context (isTestFile, isAdminRoute, isUserFacing) +
  line-level context via tree-sitter ancestor walking
- DataFlowTracer: single-file data flow analysis (passesThrough, hasPropertyInScope)
- ASTVerdict system: confirmed/suppressed/regex_only per violation with confidence

ContextAnalyzer v1 — dynamic confidence scoring:
- 5-signal weighted system: AST verdict (35%), file path (15%), framework handling (15%),
  historical FP rate (20%), user suppression rate (15%)
- Produces confidence 0.0-1.0 per violation
- CLI displays color-coded confidence badges and AST verdict badges

Framework allowlisting:
- .halorc.json integration for framework declaration
- Three profiles: Next.js, Django, Rails with per-rule suppressions/downgrades

Bug fixes:
- coppa-sec-010 FP in test files: isTestFile() guard in regex scanFile() path
- Tree-sitter native module isolation: maxWorkers in jest config

Integrated into scanFileWithAST() pipeline. 459 tests passing across 10 suites.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Optimize for large terrain — DDA raycasting, surface culling, static shadows

- DDA grid traversal replaces Three.js O(n) raycaster on every mousemove;
  now O(steps) regardless of voxel count
- Surface culling: only place voxels with at least one exposed face,
  eliminating millions of invisible interior blocks on 400×400 terrain
- Static shadow maps: autoUpdate=false, re-triggered only on add/remove
- Shadow map 4096→2048 (75% less GPU memory)
- Two-pass terrain: precompute height array, then cull per column

fix(cover): fix delete 500 error and broken image after upload

  zero out NOT NULL columns (client_id FK), which caused 0 rows affected
  and the custom "record not found" error
- Upload: use view_url from upload response for optimistic SWR update
  (revalidate: false) instead of refetching from the public endpoint
  which returns the raw S3 path, causing a broken image icon

commit.message:
feat: phase 4 — block chain verification + C-Chain sync + validator tracking

- Add verify_block_chain() to walk P-Chain from tip to genesis via parent pointers at bytes [6..38]
- Add CChainBootstrapState enum for independent C-Chain bootstrap sequence
- C-Chain bootstrap triggers after P-Chain Done: GetAccepted → GetAncestors recursive (10 rounds)
- Store C-Chain blocks in CF_BLOCKS with "c:" key prefix to separate from P-Chain
- Track p_chain_tip from AcceptedFrontier for chain verification
- Store cchain_frontier for use when P-Chain bootstrap completes
- Add ValidatorInfo struct + validators HashMap in NodeState
- Pre-populate with 2 known Fuji bootstrap validators (NodeID-7Xhw... + NodeID-MFrZ...)
- Update README: 331 P-Chain blocks, 526 C-Chain blocks, 12 simultaneous peers, full bootstrap sequence

Live test: 331 P-Chain + 526 C-Chain blocks on Fuji, 280 tests pass

perf: disable backdrop-blur, bolts, SVG borders on mobile

- DivisionCards: backdrop-blur-sm → lg:backdrop-blur-sm with opaque fallback
- DivisionCards: SVG borders + bolt elements hidden on mobile (hidden lg:block)

on mobile when multiple sections were active simultaneously.

commit.message:
feat: migrate load testing to Jetson Orin, free 4090 for QLoRA (Refs #118)

Add forjar-jetson.yaml + forjar-jetson-teardown.yaml for dedicated Jetson
Orin (aarch64, CUDA 12.6, 7.4GB unified) load testing deployment.

Architecture split: Jetson runs continuous probador load tests (apr, ollama,
llama.cpp on ports 8081-8084). 4090 freed for full-time QLoRA training,
only used for occasional deep profiling (nsys/ncu).

Spec v2.3.0 updates:
- Q6K GEMV bottleneck identified: 31.9% GPU time, 4x slower than Q4K MWV (GH #118)
- Updated baselines: APR 40.8 tok/s decode (c=4) vs llama.cpp 233.5
- PMAT-019 (Q6K MWV kernel), PMAT-020 (Jetson migration) added
- Fixed OLLAMA_MODEL tag mismatch (was q4_K_M suffix, now matches ollama list)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: migrate high-priority docs to FeatureFrame API

Update 7 docs to use new FeatureFrame.pit_join() and ctx.features():

- docs/concepts/point-in-time-joins.md: Core PIT concept doc
- docs/guides/training-to-serving.md: E2E ML workflow guide
- docs/guides/feature-store.md: Feature store quick start
- docs/building-blocks/feature-groups.md: Feature group basics
- docs/examples/featurestore.md: Feature store examples
- docs/duckdb-getting-started.md: DuckDB quickstart
- docs/reference/troubleshooting.md: Troubleshooting guide

Old API (still works, but deprecated):
- HistoricalFeaturesDuckDB + FeatureLookup + using_spine()
- OnlineFeaturesDuckDB.get_features()

New API (recommended):
- ctx.ref("feature_group.X").pit_join(spine, date_col, keep_cols)
- ctx.features("entity", ["fg.feature", ...])

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat!: dotfiles v2.0.0 upgrade with full-featured production setup

This comprehensive upgrade brings best practices for secrets management, work machine
support, idempotent scripting, cross-platform correctness, CI/CD, and container awareness.

## Phase 1: Secrets Management (age encryption)
- Add [age] section to .chezmoi.toml.tmpl with identity/recipient config
- Add CI/container environment detection ($isCI, $isCodespaces, $isContainer)
- Create scripts/setup-age-key.sh to bootstrap age key generation
- Expand docs/examples/secrets.yml.example with all secret fields

## Phase 2: Work Machine Templates
- Conditional git identity (work_email, work_signing_key) when work_machine=true
- Add [custom.work] module to starship.toml showing "󰼑 WORK" indicator
- Export WORK_MACHINE env var in all shell configs (bash, zsh, fish)

## Phase 3: Shared Chezmoi Templates
- Create .chezmoitemplates/brew-path.tmpl for DRY homebrew path logic
- Update fish config and bootstrap scripts to use template partial

## Phase 4: Fish Config Robustness
- Add `type -q` guards around all tool init calls (zoxide, fzf, direnv, atuin, starship)
- Fix alert.fish with notify-send guard and macOS osascript fallback

## Phase 5: Idempotent Bootstrap Refactoring
- Split monolithic run_once_install_default_brew_tools.sh into two scripts:
  - run_once_before_00_set_default_shell.sh.tmpl (one-time fish install + chsh)
  - run_onchange_install_brew_tools.sh.tmpl (re-runs when tools/plugins change)
- Use SHA256 hash triggers for change detection

## Phase 6: GitHub Actions CI/CD
- Create .github/workflows/ci.yml with shellcheck lint and template render tests
- Add CI badge to README.md

## Phase 7: Remove Duplicate Starship Config
- Delete dot_starship.toml (redundant; use dot_config/starship.toml)

## Phase 8: Mise Version Manager
- Create dot_config/mise/config.toml with node=lts, python=3.12
- Add mise to brew tools list

## Phase 9: Container-Aware Setup
- Add early exit in run_onchange_install_brew_tools.sh when is_container=true
- Respects $CI and $CODESPACES environment variables

## Phase 10: Miscellaneous Fixes
- Fix broken Zoxide link in site/index.html
- Remove inaccurate windows/ directory reference from CLAUDE.md
- Update shell configs with WORK_MACHINE export block
- Use GitHub privacy-preserving noreply email as git fallback

## Breaking Changes
- run_once_install_default_brew_tools.sh.tmpl removed and split
- dot_starship.toml removed (use dot_config/starship.toml)

## Verification
✓ All shell scripts pass shellcheck
✓ All templates render without error
✓ chezmoi diff works correctly
✓ GitHub Actions workflow syntax valid

BREAKING CHANGE: Bootstrap script refactored from single run_once to two scripts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Move placeholder & review posting from reviewer agent to daemon (#1771)

* feat: Move placeholder & review posting from reviewer agent to daemon

The reviewer agent previously handled posting a "Review in progress"
placeholder comment and updating it with the final review via prompt
instructions. This was fragile — some Claude models escape `!` characters
(`<\!--`), breaking the `<!-- midtown-placeholder -->` tag.

Now the daemon handles both operations:
- Posts the placeholder as an `on_success` effect when spawning a reviewer
- Updates it when the reviewer calls `midtown pr review post`

Changes:
- Add `placeholder_comment_id` field to `PrReviewerAssignment`
- New `PostPrComment` effect variant + executor
- Chain `PostPrComment` in reviewer spawn `on_success`
- Restructure CLI: `midtown pr review <N>` → `midtown pr review request <N>`
- New `midtown pr review post --pr <N> --body-file <path>` command
- New `pr.review-post` RPC handler (adds frontmatter + footer automatically)
- Optimize snapshot: three-tier placeholder lookup (stored ID → cache → API)
- Update reviewer/reviewer-resume prompts to use new CLI command

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: Add E2E tests for daemon reviewer posting

Replace the 3 manual validation steps with automated tests:

1. Placeholder body correctness (pr_tests.rs):
   - Verifies `<!-- midtown-placeholder -->` tag is exact (not escaped)
   - Asserts no `\!` characters anywhere in the body
   - Checks `> [!NOTE]` callout and Midtown footer are present

2. PostPrComment effect execution (effects_tests.rs):
   - Mocks `gh pr comment` to return a comment URL
   - Verifies comment ID is parsed from `issuecomment-<id>` format
   - Verifies comment ID is parsed from bare numeric URL format
   - Confirms ID is stored on PrReviewerAssignment
   - Confirms placeholder cache is populated

3. pr.review-post RPC handler (rpc_prs_tests.rs):
   - Error path: no assignment → returns RPC error
   - Success path: stored comment ID → mocked gh → returns success
   - Verifies placeholder cache is cleared after posting
   - Body format: frontmatter tag + user body + footer
   - Round-trip: extract_reviewer_from_pr_comments can parse the output

Also fixes 4 pre-existing test failures from the prompt changes:
- agents_tests: Updated to check for `midtown pr review post` instead of `gh pr comment`
- agents_tests: Replaced frontmatter requirement test with daemon-posting verification
- wake_reason_tests: Updated nudge template assertion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Address PR review feedback (items 1-6)

1. docs/architecture.md: Document PostPrComment effect, pr.review-post
   RPC, placeholder_comment_id field, and three-tier lookup strategy.

2. README.md: Add `midtown pr review request <N>` to CLI table.

3. Replace `expect("at least one repo path")` in handle_pr_review_post
   with a graceful RPC error return, preventing daemon panics.

4. Surface UpdatePrComment failures to the caller — inline the gh API
   PATCH call instead of routing through execute_effects() (which
   returns ()). The reviewer agent now receives errors and can retry.
   Added test: test_review_post_gh_api_failure_returns_error.

5. Fix blocking save_for_repo under async mutex in post_pr_comment —
   serialize JSON under the lock, then write to disk via spawn_blocking
   after releasing it.

6. Update stale module doc comment in rpc_prs.rs to reflect all handlers
   (prs.status, pr.review, pr.review-post, pr.merge).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add per-file leveling to Mix; update all documentation

When Loudness Norm is enabled, Mix now individually normalizes each input
file to the target LUFS (two-pass EBU R128, linear) before blending, then
runs the standard loudnorm pass on the combined output. amix uses
normalize=0 when files are pre-leveled, normalize=1 otherwise.

Updates README, manual (index.html), and theory of operation (theory.html)
to document the new mix pipeline, updated phase labels, and design rationale.

commit.message:
Implement SupportedTokenTypes() on all credential structs (#255)

* Implement SupportedTokenTypes() on all credential structs

Enables Assemble to determine at placement time which token sources
each payment gateway can process, avoiding expensive gRPC calls to
v2 connector services. Token type values aligned with v2 connector
GetDefinition and psp-proxy implementations.

* Fix TrustPayments and Woodforest token types

TrustPayments uses pcib.HvtPan() - should return PAN, not nil.
Woodforest v2 connector defines {TS_NETWORK_TOKEN, TS_PAN}.

Add fantasy GP fetcher, FA salaries, 98% test coverage, and trade deadline urgency

- Fetch real fantasy GP per position from Fantrax API (GAMES_PER_POS view),
  stored in fantasy_gp_per_position table — replaces inaccurate NHL GP sum
- Fetch player salaries from Fantrax into fantrax_players table
